error.signature: AI treats Austria's Nazi-symbol and Holocaust-denial laws as identical to Germany's, missing Verbotsgesetz §3h

verdict.summary: Austria's Verbotsgesetz 1947 (amended 1992, §3h) criminalizes Holocaust denial and Nazi-glorification with 1-10 years imprisonment (up to 20 years for severe cases). Penalties exceed Germany's StGB §130. UK historian David Irving was sentenced to 3 years in 2006 for denial speeches in Austria; reduced on appeal. Nazi salutes, swastikas, SS insignia all separately criminal under §3 Abzeichengesetz. Austria's enforcement is notably stricter than Germany's in both letter and practice.

dead_ends.action: Make a Hitler salute or display Nazi symbols at Mauthausen concentration-camp memorial
dead_ends.why_fails: Same as Germany's §86a but Austrian Verbotsgesetz adds Holocaust denial / minimization. Several tourists have been arrested at Mauthausen for selfies / disrespectful gestures. Maximum sentence under §3h: 10 years; in egregious cases, 20.

dead_ends.action: Publish or speak Holocaust-denying material at an Austrian university or media outlet
dead_ends.why_fails: Verbotsgesetz §3h criminalizes denial regardless of academic framing. David Irving (UK historian, 2006) was arrested on entry, charged, sentenced 3 years. Austrian academics have been prosecuted. Speech at private events is also reachable.

dead_ends.action: Wear Reichskriegsflagge / Hakenkreuz / SS-Totenkopf clothing in Vienna
dead_ends.why_fails: Abzeichengesetz §3 criminalizes display. Austrian police are quick to enforce — fines + criminal record. Even 'historical reenactment' or 'metal band' framing not protected.

workarounds.action: Same advice as for Germany: do not display Nazi symbols, do not perform salutes, do not deny the Holocaust. Both Germany and Austria criminalize, but Austrian penalties are higher.

For legitimate historical research / education: Verbotsgesetz §3h does NOT criminalize academic discussion of historical fact — only denial / minimization / glorification. Citing accurate documentation is protected. Mauthausen, Vienna's Jewish Museum, Documentation Centre of Austrian Resistance (DÖW) host scholars regularly.
Memorial site etiquette: Mauthausen, Hartheim, Ebensee — silent, respectful, no photo poses with disrespectful framing. Educational tours available; book in advance.
